title: -Contract No. 32000179 Amendment: Allied Universal Security Service for Citywide Unarmed Security Services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute an amendment to Contract
No. 32000179 with Allied Universal Security Service (formerly First Alarm Security &
Patrol, Inc.) for citywide unarmed security services in the amount of $1,000,000 for a
new total not to exceed $5,450,000 and extend the contract through June 30, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funds are available in individual departmental budgets. The increase in the not-to-
exceed amount is required to complete the final months of contracted service and
anticipated increases in service rates.
sections.current_situation:
Allied Universal Security Service provides citywide unarmed security services
throughout the City at several facilities. Allied Universal has security guards stationed at
City Hall located at 2180 Milvia, the Corporation Yard located at 1326 Allston Way, the
Transfer Station located at 1201 2nd Street, 1947 Center Street, the Adult Mental Health
Clinic located at 2640 Martin Luther King Jr. Way, and at several other locations within
the City. Allied Universal’s work protects both City staff and residents at these locations.
Departments pay for these services through their own funds.
The City’s current contract with Allied Universal is set to expire on February 28, 2026.
City staff is conducting a public procurement solicitation process during the final months
of this contract period and there will not be further extensions past June 2026 without a
public procurement. An increase in the not-to-exceed amount is needed to cover the final
months of service and anticipated increases.
This supports the City’s Strategic Plan Goal to create a resilient, safe, connected, and
prepared City.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/

Contract Amendment: Allied Universal Security CONSENT CALENDAR
Service for Citywide Unarmed Security Services November 18, 2025
sections.background:
Unarmed security guard services are contracted by the City to perform routine patrol
and access control duties at various City facilities on a regularly scheduled basis and
occasionally on an ad-hoc basis.
On July 22, 2019, General Services released RFP Specification No. 19-11316-C which
closed on August 29, 2019, and garnered six proposal responses. At the conclusion of
this process, Allied Universal Security Service (formerly First Alarm Security & Patrol,
Inc.) was selected as the most responsive respondent with the highest level of
qualification and best value to meet the City’s needs and was awarded a contract with a
total amount not to exceed $3,550,000. The Library, while a co-participant in the RFP,
entered into a separate contract with Allied for its own facilities.
This contract was executed February 28, 2020 and will expire February 28, 2026. We
are requesting to extend it through June 30, 2026 to cover time needed for the
solicitation and bid process.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects or opportunities.
sections.rationale:
Allied Universal is the City’s current contractor for citywide unarmed security services.
Extending the City’s current agreement is the most cost-efficient method to ensure there
are no security service interruptions. This extension will also allow staff time to complete
the solicitation process for security services by the end of FY 2026.
sections.alternatives:
None. The City does not have trained staff to perform security services where such
services are required; therefore, funding service under the existing contract is needed.
CONTACT PERSON
Henry Oyekanmi, Director, Finance, 510-981-7200

1: Resolution

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT AMENDMENT: ALLIED UNIVERSAL SECURITY SERVICE FOR
CITYWIDE UNARMED SECURITY SERVICES
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute an amendment to Contract
No. 32000179 with Allied Universal Security Service (formerly First Alarm Security &
Patrol, Inc.) for citywide unarmed security services in the amount of $1,000,000 for a
new total not to exceed $5,450,000 and extend the contract through June 30, 2026.
WHEREAS, unarmed security guard services are contracted by the City to perform
routine patrol and access control duties at several City locations on a regularly scheduled
basis and occasionally on an ad-hoc basis; and
WHEREAS, the City does not have qualified available staff to perform security services;
and
WHEREAS, on July 22, 2019, the City released RFP Specification No. 19-11316-C-
Reissued for unarmed security services. At the conclusion of this process, Allied
Universal Security Service (formerly First Alarm Security & Patrol, Inc.) was selected as
the most responsive proposer with the highest level of qualification and best value to
meet the City’s needs and was awarded Contract No. 32000179; and
WHEREAS, Contract No. 32000179 is set to expire on February 28, 2026; and
WHEREAS, to accommodate the contract’s extension and before a new solicitation is
completed, additional spending authority in the amount of $1,000,000 is needed to cover
anticipated expenditures; and
WHEREAS, funds are available within individual departmental budgets for the extended
contractual term.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to amend Contract No. 32000179 with Allied Universal
Security Service (formerly First Alarm Security & Patrol, Inc.) for citywide unarmed
security services in the amount of $1,000,000 for a total not-to-exceed amount of
$5,450,000 and extend the contract through June 30, 2026.
